project.name: LBM
project.summary: Local Business Manager v1 — standalone, self-contained browser-based task tracker. Open index.html to get started.

tasks.title: Table view
Multiple views for bulk edits
Select and delete multiple tasks
Work on the latest recommended updates by Claude to publish
tasks.notes: LBM-S001 P1 — Remove legacy Mac Control Center task references from seed dataLBM-S002 P1 — Add screenshots and visual walkthroughLBM-S003 P1 — Cross-browser compatibility testingLBM-S004 P2 — Verify all internal doc links are correctLBM-S005 P2 — Create CONTRIBUTING.md for open-source handoff
tasks.body:
• LBM-S001 P1 — Remove legacy Mac Control Center task references from seed data
  • LBM-S002 P1 — Add screenshots and visual walkthrough
  • LBM-S003 P1 — Cross-browser compatibility testing
  • LBM-S004 P2 — Verify all internal doc links are correct
  • LBM-S005 P2 — Create CONTRIBUTING.md for open-source handoff

tasks.title: create website for clients
Create and plan the website for developers
add board level history
add history for all actions / tasks
show default properties of each actions in the expanded view
Update the expanded view of actions
Add CRUD operations for the documents tab

the design of the delete pop-up doesn't look very good. We need to make sure it is aligned with our look and feel and design. Other than that, it's good. Let's make sure we fix this!   Let's make sure you can use the keyboard navigation arrows to be able to move around to cancel or delete or escape out of it.   Let's make sure, across the board, that if we press Escape, we come out. For example, if we're searching, if you press Escape, we come out of focus shifts. Basically, if we open up any of the items in the toolbar, if you press Escape, we basically come out logically across the board, across the app.
Introduce Notion like blocks for the body of the cards.
Updated how tasks are entered.
Make tasks auto focus on title when we are creating.
❇️ Add a delete button on the list view.
Define the local-to-online tracker sync workflow
tasks.notes: Choose the online system, decide whether the repo or the online tracker is the source of truth, and build a lightweight export/import habit before automation.
tasks.body: Choose the online system, decide whether the repo or the online tracker is the source of truth, and build a lightweight export/import habit before automation.

tasks.title: Add duplicate shortcut validation for action creation and editing
tasks.notes: Prevent or warn on `Cmd + [Key]` conflicts so one action does not silently shadow another.
tasks.body: Prevent or warn on `Cmd + [Key]` conflicts so one action does not silently shadow another.

tasks.title: Add import, export, and reset controls for action config
tasks.notes: Expose safe backup and restore flows for `config.json` from the app UI instead of requiring filesystem work.
tasks.body: Expose safe backup and restore flows for `config.json` from the app UI instead of requiring filesystem work.

tasks.title: Replace AppleScript launch-at-login with a modern macOS approach
tasks.notes: Review ServiceManagement-based options and reduce brittleness around login items.
tasks.body: Review ServiceManagement-based options and reduce brittleness around login items.

tasks.title: Make the quick-launch dock user-configurable
tasks.notes: The dock is currently hardcoded to Chrome, ChatGPT, and Telegram. Move that list into user-managed configuration.

tasks.title: Improve action failure feedback beyond notifications only
tasks.notes: Add inline error clarity, last-run status, or lightweight execution history so failures are easier to diagnose.

tasks.title: Add a GitHub Actions smoke-build workflow
tasks.notes: Run `./build.sh` on macOS in CI before adding release automation.
tasks.body: Run `./build.sh` on macOS in CI before adding release automation.

tasks.title: Package and publish a repeatable release artifact
tasks.notes: Decide whether the first public release should be a `.zip` or `.dmg`, then document and script the packaging flow.

tasks.title: Define a trusted-command model for shell actions
tasks.notes: Document what kinds of shell commands are expected, how users should store scripts, and how to avoid unsafe defaults in a public repo.

tasks.title: Create screenshots and first-run onboarding docs
tasks.notes: Capture the app modes and action setup flow so the repo is easier for future collaborators to understand quickly.

tasks.title: Add config schema migration and backup planning
tasks.notes: Before expanding settings and action metadata, decide how old config files will be migrated safely.

tasks.title: Review branding polish for iconography and status-item presentation
tasks.notes: The app currently uses a text bolt in the menu bar. Consider a stronger asset and consistency pass after core product work stabilizes.
tasks.body: The app currently uses a text bolt in the menu bar. Consider a stronger asset and consistency pass after core product work stabilizes.

tasks.title: Add basic automated coverage for config normalization and persistence
tasks.notes: Even lightweight verification around `ConfigManager` would reduce regressions when action metadata grows.
tasks.body: Even lightweight verification around `ConfigManager` would reduce regressions when action metadata grows.

tasks.title: Add keyboard shortcuts: N to create, / to search, Esc to close
tasks.notes: Basic hotkeys make the tracker feel native and reduce friction for power users. N opens the add-task modal, / focuses the search input, Esc dismisses any open modal.
tasks.body: Basic hotkeys make the tracker feel native and reduce friction for power users. N opens the add-task modal, / focuses the search input, Esc dismisses any open modal.

tasks.title: Shorten export filenames and include date timestamp
tasks.notes: Current filenames are long and generic. Switching to mcc-tracker-YYYY-MM-DD.json and mcc-tracker-YYYY-MM-DD.md makes exports easier to sort and identify.
tasks.body: Current filenames are long and generic. Switching to mcc-tracker-YYYY-MM-DD.json and mcc-tracker-YYYY-MM-DD.md makes exports easier to sort and identify.
